findings.ruleDescription: Detects hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, and credentials in skill files. 10.9% of ClawHub skills contained hardcoded credentials (Snyk research). May indicate stolen credentials, test keys left in production, or intentional backdoors.
findings.recommendation: This skill contains what appears to be a hardcoded API key, token, or password. This could be: (1) stolen credentials embedded for exfiltration, (2) test credentials accidentally left in code, or (3) an intentional backdoor. Review carefully before use. If you've already installed, rotate any exposed credentials immediately.

matches.description: Generic API key or secret assignment with realistic-looking value

fullDescription.text: Detects hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, and credentials in skill files. 10.9% of ClawHub skills contained hardcoded credentials (Snyk research). May indicate stolen credentials, test keys left in production, or intentional backdoors.

This could be: (1) stolen credentials embedded for exfiltration, (2) test credentials accidentally left in code, or (3) an intentional backdoor. Review carefully before use. If you've already installed, rotate any exposed credentials immediately.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests to domains that don't match the tool's declared purpose. MCP servers making unexpected network calls may be exfiltrating data or phoning home to C2 infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Review all network calls. Verify each URL belongs to the service the tool is supposed to interact with. Unexpected POST requests are the highest-risk pattern.

matches.description: fetch() with POST method and body containing collected data

fullDescription.text: Detects outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests to domains that don't match the tool's declared purpose. MCP servers making unexpected network calls may be exfiltrating data or phoning home to C2 infrastructure.

matchDetails.description: fetch() with POST method and body containing collected data

properties.recommendation: Review all network calls. Verify each URL belongs to the service the tool is supposed to interact with. Unexpected POST requests are the highest-risk pattern.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects MCP servers that bind to localhost or 0.0.0.0 without authentication. CVE-2025-49596 exploited this in MCP Inspector where the inspector-proxy listened on localhost without authentication, allowing RCE.
findings.recommendation: This server binds to a network interface without apparent authentication. CVE-2025-49596 demonstrated how an unauthenticated localhost listener in MCP Inspector allowed remote code execution. Always add authentication and restrict binding to localhost with auth checks.

matches.description: Server listening on 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces) — exposed to network

fullDescription.text: Detects MCP servers that bind to localhost or 0.0.0.0 without authentication. CVE-2025-49596 exploited this in MCP Inspector where the inspector-proxy listened on localhost without authentication, allowing RCE.

findings.ruleTitle: File size anomaly for file type
findings.ruleDescription: Flags files whose size significantly exceeds the expected range for their file type. A tailwind.config.js at 7KB when typical is 1-2KB, or a SKILL.md at 50KB when typical is 2-5KB, suggests hidden content.
findings.reportTitle: File size or structure anomaly detected
findings.recommendation: Inspect the file for hidden content, especially at the end of long lines or after the apparent end of meaningful code.

matches.description: Config file more than 8x expected size for its type
